dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T are four near-identical classes (~300 lines each) with copy-paste method implementations. A base class or mixin pattern would eliminate 900+ lines of duplication.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: search.py at 1880 lines violates the 1000-line limit and contains duplicated patterns. The _search_query helper is duplicated in both search.py and engine.py. The _Result and _MetaResult type aliases are also duplicated.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Heavy use of Any across process.py, engine.py, and search.py parameters. Callable types are insufficient — scorer protocols should be defined. Return types use unsafe union patterns (list[_Result] | list[_MetaResult]).
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: BM25L.__init__ has a duplicate assignment 'self._normalize_scores = normalize_scores' (line 533). BM25Plus has the same defect (line 848). The get_top_n_reranked method is copy-pasted across all 4 BM25 variants with identical blend logic.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: engine.py and search.py both define _search_query, _Result, and _MetaResult independently. The Retriever class accesses private attributes of HybridSearch (_corpus, _metadata, _embeddings) creating tight coupling.
dimension_notes.package_organization: search.py is a monolith containing 4 BM25 classes, HybridSearch, Reranker, and helper functions. This should be split into separate modules per class.

issues.summary: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T are near-identical classes with copy-pasted methods — ~900 duplicated lines
evidence: BM25 (lines 71-510), BM25L (lines 513-825), BM25Plus (lines 828-1140), BM25T share get_top_n, get_top_n_fuzzy, get_top_n_rrf, fuzzy_only, explain, get_idf, get_document_vector, add_documents, remove_documents, get_top_n_reranked, get_top_n_phrase, async wrappers, and fluent builder methods with identical implementations
issues.suggestion: Extract a BaseBM25 class containing all shared methods. Each variant subclass only overrides __init__ to call the correct _rustfuzz constructor (BM25Index, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T). The shared ~25 methods can be inherited.

issues.summary: search.py at 1880 lines exceeds stated 1000 line limit and contains too many responsibilities
evidence: search.py contains BM25 (71-510), BM25L (513-825), BM25Plus (828-1140), BM25T (1145-1455), HybridSearch (1458-1750), Reranker class, helper functions _enrich, _build_corpus_index, _validate_metadata, and _search_query
issues.suggestion: After extracting a BaseBM25 base class, split into bm25_base.py (base class + helpers), bm25_variants.py (BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T subclasses), hybrid.py (HybridSearch), and reranker.py.

issues.summary: BM25L.__init__ assigns self._normalize_scores twice on adjacent lines
evidence: Line 532-533: 'self._normalize_scores = normalize_scores' appears twice in BM25L.__init__
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate assignment on line 533.

issues.summary: BM25Plus.__init__ assigns self._normalize_scores twice on adjacent lines
evidence: Lines 847-848: 'self._normalize_scores = normalize_scores' appears twice in BM25Plus.__init__
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate assignment on line 848.

issues.summary: _search_query helper function is defined identically in both search.py and engine.py
evidence: search.py line 26-29 defines '_search_query(owner)' with identical lazy import of SearchQuery; engine.py line 156-160 has the same definition
issues.suggestion: Define _search_query in one location (search.py or a shared module) and import it where needed.

issues.summary: _Result and _MetaResult type aliases are defined identically in search.py and engine.py
evidence: search.py lines 21-22 defines '_Result = tuple[str, float]' and '_MetaResult = tuple[str, float, Any]'; engine.py lines 55-56 has identical definitions
issues.suggestion: Define these type aliases in a single location and import them.

issues.summary: Excessive use of Any in function signatures where more specific Protocol types could be used
evidence: process.py uses 'Callable[..., float]' for scorer but should use a Protocol with specific signature; engine.py's __init__ uses 'corpus: Iterable[str] | Iterable[Any] | Any' which is equivalent to just Any; search.py uses 'reranker: Any' throughout
issues.suggestion: Define Protocol classes for Scorer, Reranker, and EmbeddingCallback. Use overloads for methods that return different types based on metadata presence.

issues.summary: Retriever accesses private _corpus, _metadata, _embeddings attributes of HybridSearch and BM25
evidence: engine.py line 548: 'self._corpus = self._hybrid._corpus'; line 549: 'self._metadata = self._hybrid._metadata'; line 779: 'emb_for_pickle = self._hybrid._embeddings'
issues.suggestion: Expose corpus, metadata as read-only properties on BM25/HybridSearch and access via the public interface.

issues.summary: get_top_n_reranked blend logic (~30 lines) is copy-pasted across BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T
evidence: BM25.get_top_n_reranked (333-383), BM25L.get_top_n_reranked (661-711), BM25Plus.get_top_n_reranked (974-1024) share identical blend alpha logic
issues.suggestion: Extract the blend logic into a standalone function _blend_reranked_scores and call it from each class (or better, from the shared base class).

issues.summary: from_column class methods have inconsistent return type annotations across BM25 variants
evidence: BM25.from_column returns '-> BM25', BM25L.from_column returns '-> BM25L', BM25Plus returns '-> BM25Plus' — using Self would be more consistent and support subclassing
issues.suggestion: Use typing.Self (Python 3.11+) or TypeVar for from_column return types to ensure consistency.

issues.summary: BM25.explain calls self._corpus.index(doc) which raises ValueError with an unhelpful message when doc is not found
evidence: BM25.explain line 256: 'idx = self._corpus.index(doc)' raises 'ValueError: ... is not in list' — no domain-specific error message
issues.suggestion: Catch the ValueError and raise a more descriptive error: 'Document not found in corpus'.

issues.summary: Tests exist for individual metrics but no test verifies API parity across BM25 variants
evidence: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T should all support the same methods with the same signatures, but no parametrized test enforces this contract
issues.suggestion: Add a parametrized test that instantiates each variant and verifies all common methods exist and return correct types.

issues.summary: WRatio and QRatio use PascalCase while all other functions use snake_case
evidence: fuzz.py exports WRatio and QRatio alongside snake_case functions like partial_ratio, token_sort_ratio
issues.suggestion: These names maintain backward compatibility with rapidfuzz/fuzzywuzzy. Consider adding snake_case aliases (w_ratio, q_ratio) alongside the existing names.

issues.summary: Scorer resolution logic (_scorer, scorer_name, scorer_obj) is repeated 4 times in process.py
evidence: Lines 34-39, 82-84, 106-108, 139-141 repeat the same scorer resolution pattern
issues.suggestion: Extract a _resolve_scorer(scorer) helper that returns (scorer_name, scorer_obj) tuple.
